data.content:
Here's my analysis of the legacy patterns and the modernized replacement:

**Legacy patterns found:**

| # | Pattern | Resolution |
|---|---------|-----------|
| 1 | Non-SDK `<Project>` with `ToolsVersion` and `xmlns` | Use `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">` |
| 2 | Explicit `Microsoft.Common.props` / `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` imports | Handled by SDK automatically |
| 3 | `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic` | Unnecessary — SDK defaults |
| 4 | Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` blocks | SDK provides sensible defaults |
| 5 | Explicit `<Reference>` for System assemblies | Implicit in SDK-style .NET Framework projects |
| 6 | Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items | SDK-style globs all `*.cs` automatically |
| 7 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Replaced by `<PropertyGroup>` metadata |
| 8 | `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | Use `TargetFramework net472` |

Now I'll apply the modernization:

arguments.old_str:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Remove obsolete AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Remove obsolete AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a complete picture of the legacy project. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and explain how to modernize it to SDK-style format.

Legacy patterns identified:
1. **Non-SDK project format** - Uses `ToolsVersion="15.0"` and explicit `xmlns` declaration instead of `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">`
2. **Explicit Microsoft.Common.props import** - SDK-style handles this automatically
3. **Explicit Microsoft.CSharp.targets import** - SDK-style handles this automatically
4. **Configuration/Platform property groups with defaults** - SDK provides sensible defaults


5. **ProjectGuid, FileAlignment, AutoGenerateBindingRedirects, and Deterministic** - All unnecessary in SDK-style since they're either defaults or handled automatically
6. **Debug/Release configurations** - SDK provides built-in defaults for debug symbols, optimization levels, output paths, and warning settings
7. **Explicit system assembly references and Compile includes** - SDK-style handles these implicitly, so they can be removed entirely
8. **AssemblyInfo.cs** - I can replace this with MSBuild properties like `GenerateAssemblyInfo` directly in the project file, then delete the separate file
9. **TargetFrameworkVersion** - This becomes `TargetFramework` with the modern syntax like `net472`

Now I'm going to modernize the project file itself.

    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>

data.content:
Done. The project went from **47 lines → 13 lines**. Here's what was removed/simplified:

- **Deleted**: `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` — metadata now lives in `<PropertyGroup>` (auto-generated at build)
- **Removed**: Explicit `<Compile>` items — SDK globs `*.cs` automatically
- **Removed**: System assembly `<Reference>` items — implicit for `net472`
- **Removed**: Debug/Release property groups — SDK provides correct defaults
- **Removed**: `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `OutputType` (Library is the default)
- **Removed**: MSBuild imports — the `Sdk` attribute handles everything

The source files (`Calculator.cs`, `StringHelper.cs`) are unchanged and will compile identically.
